name: Remote MCP GitHub Token Read
description: Single-stage workflow that proves a specialist can use a token-authenticated GitHub remote MCP server to inspect the fixtures repository.
outcome: A single specialist uses GitHub MCP with a token-backed header and returns deterministic repository facts.

definition.process_instructions:
Run the workflow through the authored stage `remote-repository-read`. Treat that stage as the only workflow checkpoint: `remote-repository-read` exists to prove that a specialist can use a granted remote MCP server to inspect a repository and close the work with deterministic facts. Use this role deliberately: `github-repo-inspector` owns the entire work item. The orchestrator only needs to route that specialist, then close the work when the specialist has produced the artifact and a structured handoff.
Happy path: dispatch `github-repo-inspector`, require the specialist to use the granted GitHub MCP tools against the `agirunner/agirunner-test-fixtures` repository, write `deliverables/github-repo-proof.md`, upload it as an artifact, and submit one structured handoff that repeats the final answer. The final result MUST include `Repository: agirunner-test-fixtures`, `Default branch: main`, and `README present: yes`.
If the specialist cannot reach the remote MCP server, cannot inspect the repository, or tries to bypass the remote MCP capability, require a corrective retry or escalate with a precise reason. Do not silently substitute another tool path, do not invent reviews or approvals, and do not close the workflow without proof that a remote MCP tool was actually used. Do not keep the workflow open for non-blocking formatting noise once the repository facts and artifact exist; record that as a callout instead.
Completion rules: finish only after `github-repo-inspector` has produced the artifact, repeated the repository facts in the final handoff, and left a usable proof packet. Guided closure rule: treat the authored flow as best-intent process guidance. Preferred use of the granted remote MCP server must be followed, but the orchestrator may waive a weaker preferred detail with a recorded reason when the legal remote-MCP-backed answer already exists. Smaller deviations must be recorded as callouts instead of preventing closure.

columns.label: Planned
columns.description: Work is defined but not yet assigned.

columns.label: Blocked
columns.description: Work is blocked and needs intervention.

columns.label: Done
columns.description: The work item meets its acceptance criteria.

stages.name: remote-repository-read
stages.goal: Deterministic repository facts are delivered through remote MCP.
